Keywords: Build the project with debug or release optimizations
Command: Every time you need to compile; use --release for production; use --target for cross-compilation
When To Use: Compiles to target/debug or target/release; reports errors and warnings; shows build time with --timings

Keywords: Run all unit tests, integration tests, and doc tests
Description:
cargo test
cargo test --lib
cargo test --test integration
cargo test -- --nocapture
cargo test -- test_name
Command: After every code change; before committing; in CI pipelines
When To Use: Reports pass/fail for each test; shows stdout with --nocapture; returns exit code for CI

Keywords: Run Clippy linter for catching common mistakes and non-idiomatic code
Description:
cargo clippy
cargo clippy -- -D warnings
cargo clippy -- -W clippy::pedantic
cargo clippy --fix
Command: On every commit; in CI pipeline; before code review
When To Use: Lists warnings with explanations and suggestions; --fix applies automatic fixes; exit code for CI

Keywords: Format code according to Rust style guidelines
Command: Before every commit; in CI to enforce style; configure rustfmt.toml for team standards
When To Use: Formats all Rust files in place; --check returns non-zero if changes needed; respects rustfmt.toml

Keywords: Generate HTML documentation for the project and all dependencies
Command: To review API documentation; to check doc tests compile; before publishing a crate
When To Use: Generates HTML docs in target/doc/; opens in browser with --open; builds doc tests

Keywords: Check dependencies for known security vulnerabilities
Command: Before deployment; in CI pipeline; after adding new dependencies
When To Use: Lists CVEs affecting dependencies; suggests upgrade paths; JSON output for automation

Keywords: Lint dependencies for security, licensing, and duplicate issues
Description:
cargo install cargo-deny
cargo deny check
cargo deny check licenses
cargo deny check advisories
Command: In CI to enforce dependency policies; for compliance checks; to prevent banned crates
When To Use: Reports license violations, security advisories, duplicate crates; uses deny.toml config

Keywords: A faster test runner that executes tests in parallel with better output
Description:
cargo install cargo-nextest
cargo nextest run
cargo nextest run --retries 2
cargo nextest run --partition count:1/3
Command: For large test suites; in CI for faster feedback; for test retries and partitioning
When To Use: Faster parallel execution; per-test timing; retries; JUnit XML output; test partitioning for CI

Category: miri, undefined behavior, unsafe, memory, UB
Keywords: Detect undefined behavior in unsafe code including memory errors
Description: rustup component add miri\ncargo miri test\ncargo miri run
Command: After writing or modifying unsafe code; for checking FFI boundaries; for validating custom allocators
When To Use: Detects use-after-free, double-free, out-of-bounds, uninitialized memory, data races in unsafe code

Keywords: Expand all macros to see the generated code
Description:
cargo install cargo-expand
cargo expand
cargo expand module_name
cargo expand --theme dracula
Command: When debugging macro errors; to understand derived trait implementations; when writing proc macros
When To Use: Shows fully expanded Rust code with all macros resolved; syntax highlighted; helps debug complex derives

Keywords: Automatically rebuild or test when files change
Description:
cargo install cargo-watch
cargo watch -x run
cargo watch -x test
cargo watch -x 'clippy -- -D warnings'
Command: During development for fast iteration; run tests on save; run clippy on save
When To Use: Watches for file changes; reruns specified command; supports chaining multiple commands

Keywords: Generate CPU flamegraphs for performance profiling
Description: cargo install flamegraph\ncargo flamegraph\ncargo flamegraph --bin myapp -- args
Command: When optimizing performance; to identify CPU hotspots; before and after optimization
When To Use: Generates interactive SVG flamegraph; shows call stack frequencies; identifies hot functions

Command: When cleaning up dependencies; before releases; after major refactoring
When To Use: Lists dependencies in Cargo.toml that are not used by any source file; reduces compile time

Command: For thorough dependency analysis; complements cargo machete; for CI checks
When To Use: More accurate than machete (uses compiler data); requires nightly; reports unused normal and dev deps

Keywords: Automate version bumping, tagging, and publishing
Description:
cargo install cargo-release
cargo release patch --dry-run
cargo release minor
cargo release --workspace
Command: When publishing crates; for version management; for workspace releases
When To Use: Bumps version in Cargo.toml; creates git tag; publishes to crates.io; handles workspace members
